Motion For An Order That The Truth Of Any Matters Specified In Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted; For An Order Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories; For An Order Compelling Responses To The Demand For Documents, For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Dec-13-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: NO APPEARANCES. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff's Motion For An Order That The Truth Of Any Matters Specified In Requests For Admission Be Deemed Admitted; For An Order Compelling Responses To Form Interrogatories; and For An Order Compelling Responses To The Demand For Documents is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant's failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. The Requests for Admission are hereby deemed admitted. Defendant is ordered to respond to the Form Interrogatories and Demand For Production of Documents within 15 days of notice of this order. Sanctions are awarded against Defendant in the amount of $386.30, to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order Compelling Access For Physical Inspection Of Real Property , Or In The Alternatvie, Compelling Response To Physical Inspection Demand Of Real Property And For An Order Imposing Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-08-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
William E. Gilg, appeared for Plaintiff/moving party
No other appearance.
Ruling:
Hearing held. Plaintiff's motion for an order compelling access for physical inspection of real property is granted. Sanctions is granted in the amount of $581.30. Order signed in open court. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
